strengths: M.Tech in AI with thesis directly on LLMs and LoRA fine-tuning

summary: Sneha is a standout candidate whose M.Tech thesis and research internship at IIT Madras directly address the core requirements of this role. Her end-to-end LLM deployment experience and production RAG work make her the strongest technical fit in this batch.

Strong internship at Ola Electric with measurable production impact
Excellent communicator and portfolio presence (personal website, 300+ users)
gaps: Less research depth compared to Sneha Iyer
No cloud certification yet
summary: Riya is an exceptional fresh graduate who has already shipped production AI systems. Her LangChain internship, open-source work, and RAG chatbot deployments make her nearly as strong as Sneha with arguably better team fit for an early-stage product company.

strengths: IIT Hyderabad with strong CGPA and Kaggle track record

Published Hugging Face model with community traction
gaps: Internship was short (3 months); no LLM API experience in production
Less exposure to newer LLM frameworks vs Riya and Sneha

Good Kaggle performance and research reading habits
Clean, modular code practices mentioned
Production-adjacent internship at Infosys
gaps: No LLM API or RAG experience
No Docker/cloud deployment experience
Tier-3 university; CGPA could be higher
summary: Vivek shows solid NLP and ML fundamentals with decent project breadth, but lacks the LLM/RAG and deployment experience the role prioritises. He's a trainable candidate who would need 2–3 months of ramp-up before contributing independently.

Google TensorFlow Developer Certification

summary: Divya has strong practical ML skills in computer vision and deployment, but her background is more hardware/CV-oriented than the NLP/LLM focus this role requires. She's a solid hire for a CV role but needs upskilling for this position.

Datathon winner — competitive mindset

summary: Priya has excellent analytical foundations and a high academic record, but her skill set is closer to a data analyst than an ML engineer. With dedicated upskilling in PyTorch and LLMs she could grow into the role, but she's not ready for it today.

No internship or real-world data project
summary: Meera's strong mathematics background is genuinely valuable long-term, but she is too early in her AI/ML learning journey for this role. She would be better suited for an entry-level data internship or a structured bootcamp before applying to engineer roles.

strengths: B.Tech in IT — correct educational direction
Shows initiative through self-learning
gaps: Very limited technical skills — no deep learning or NLP experience

summary: Karan is an early-stage learner who has not yet developed the skills required for this role. His projects are exploratory exercises rather than demonstrable competencies. He should build more substantial projects and complete the Andrew Ng ML course before re-applying.

strengths: Strong backend engineering skills (Node.js, REST APIs, databases)
Good software engineering practices (testing, deployment)

summary: Rohan is a competent backend developer but has applied to the wrong role. None of his projects or skills map to ML engineering. He should be redirected to a backend/full-stack engineering role where his skills are genuinely competitive.

strengths: Strong analytical mindset from finance background
Genuine motivation to transition into AI
Domain expertise in finance could be valuable in fintech AI contexts
gaps: 10 years in finance — career pivot is a significant risk for a startup team

summary: Amit's motivation is admirable but he is at the very beginning of a long transition journey. Without any completed ML projects or demonstrated technical output, there is too much risk and ramp-up cost for a junior engineering role at this stage.
